 {"id":"gt-5wb7","title":"Update vision.md with proto/mol/wisp terminology","description":"Update the Steam Engine Metaphor section to use consistent phase terminology:

Current (vision.md):
- Proto molecules = fuel (templates)
- Wisps = steam (transient execution traces)  
- Digests = distillate (permanent records)

New terminology:
- **Proto** = crystal/solid - frozen template
- **Mol** = liquid - reified durable instance (tracked in git)
- **Wisp** = gas - ephemeral instance (evaporates after squash)
- **Digest** = distillate - compressed summary after squash

Key clarification needed:
- Proto → bond → creates either Mol (durable) or Wisp (ephemeral)
- The choice of Mol vs Wisp depends on --wisp flag
- Default is Mol (durable, recorded in main beads)
- Wisp lives in .beads-ephemeral/, squashes away","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:32:45.68234-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T02:14:01.632565-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T02:14:01.632565-08:00","close_reason":"Already documented in vision.md and architecture.md","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-5wb7","depends_on_id":"gt-62hm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:33:17.307488-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-5wtw","title":"Shutdown request handler","description":"Handle polecat shutdown requests:

When polecat runs 'gt handoff --shutdown':
1. Polecat sends mail to <rig>/witness requesting shutdown
2. Witness receives mail
3. Witness verifies:
   - Git working tree is clean
   - Work is submitted (MR exists or in queue)
   - No uncommitted beads changes
4. If clean:
   - gt session stop <rig>/<polecat>
   - git worktree remove polecats/<name>
   - git branch -d polecat/<name>
5. If not clean:
   - Send nudge back to polecat
   - Track retry count

Depends on fixing gt-dsfi (handoff deadlock).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:14:22.968711-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T09:28:15.512143-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T09:28:15.512143-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-5wtw","depends_on_id":"gt-53w6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:14:37.300578-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-5wtw","depends_on_id":"gt-mxyj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:14:38.893061-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"gt-5xph","title":"Document session cycling protocol in all templates","description":"Add explicit lifecycle request protocol to all agent templates.

## Problem
Templates mention 'request session refresh' but don't show HOW.
Agents don't know the protocol for requesting a cycle.

## Protocol to document
1. Write handoff mail to self (for continuity)
2. Set requesting_cycle=true in state.json
3. Send LIFECYCLE mail to deacon/:
   Subject: 'LIFECYCLE: <role> requesting cycle'
   Body: Reason for cycle request

## Templates to update
- prompts/roles/polecat.md
- prompts/roles/crew.md  
- prompts/roles/witness.md
- prompts/roles/refinery.md

## Also add
- Example state.json location for each role
- When to request cycle (context full, work complete, etc.)
- What happens after (daemon kills, respawns, new session primes)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T16:43:54.10282-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T01:19:14.072793-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-5y5p","title":"Preflight molecule: verify baseline health before work","description":"Before assigning work, verify baseline (main branch) is healthy.

**From VC**: Self-healing state machine (HEALTHY → SELF_HEALING → ESCALATED). ~200 lines.

**Gas Town implementation**: Preflight molecule or refinery feature:
```yaml
preflight:
  gates: [test, lint, build]
  on_failure: create-fix-issue
```

Run at session start. If baseline broken, file a P0 fix issue and work on that first.

**Value**: Self-healing baseline. Agents don't start from broken state.

**VC lesson**: Prevents cascading failures. Agent shouldn't start work on broken code.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T20:30:13.807641-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T20:30:13.807641-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-5y5p","depends_on_id":"gt-zhpa","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-20T20:30:27.469804-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-62hm","title":"Molecule Phase Terminology Documentation","description":"Document the three-phase molecule lifecycle with consistent terminology:

## The Phases (States of Matter)

| Phase | State | Nature |
|-------|-------|--------|
| **Proto** | Solid/Crystal | Frozen template, static definition in catalog |
| **Mol** | Liquid | Reified instance, malleable, tracked in git (durable) |
| **Wisp** | Gas | Evaporates after squash, ephemeral orchestration |

## Documentation Gaps Identified

### High Priority
1. Update vision.md Steam Engine metaphor to use proto/mol/wisp terminology
2. Add lifecycle diagram: Proto → bond → Mol or Wisp → squash → Digest
3. Document Go types for phases (ProtoMolecule, etc.)
4. Document .beads-ephemeral/ structure and purpose

### Medium Priority
5. Update CLAUDE.md files (polecat, witness, refinery) with molecule workflow
6. Document bd mol bond/squash/burn CLI API with examples
7. Add polecat guide: Executing molecules and generating summaries

### Low Priority
8. Add terminology glossary to architecture.md
9. Create troubleshooting playbook for stuck molecules
10. Add reference diagrams for state transitions

## Current State
- architecture.md has good molecule basics but uses inconsistent terminology
- vision.md has Steam Engine metaphor (proto=fuel, wisp=steam, digest=distillate)
- molecules.md has detailed reference but needs phase clarity
- builtin_molecules.go has 8 molecules but no phase documentation in code","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:32:27.537487-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T02:14:33.226923-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T02:14:33.226923-08:00","close_reason":"All children closed - documentation complete"}

 {"id":"gt-7we","title":"Swarms of One: Lightweight single-worker task dispatch","description":"Design and implement a lightweight pattern for firing off single workers to handle tasks without full swarm overhead.

## Context

Currently we have:
- town spawn: Creates a polecat in a rig with issue assignment
- Swarms (sw-*): Full lifecycle tracking with manifest, state, events, reports
- Ephemeral rigs (rig-*): Temporary worker groups for swarms

What's missing: A simple way to say 'fire off a worker to do X' without swarm ceremony.

## Design Questions

1. Should this be a new command like 'gt fire' or an option on existing commands?
2. Should single tasks still get swarm IDs (sw-N) for consistency/queryability?
3. Should it default to creating new workers or support --reuse for idle polecats?
4. How does this relate to crew workers (gt-cik)?

## Possible Interface

gt fire --rig gastown --issue gt-xyz [--prompt '...']
gt fire gastown/QuickTask --issue gt-xyz

## Related

- gt-cik: Overseer Crew (user-managed persistent workspaces)
- gt-kmn: Swarm System epic","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T16:51:02.716629-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:18.589027-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:18.589027-08:00"}
+{"id":"gt-7xtn","title":"Bug: MRs appearing in bd ready output","description":"merge-request beads are showing up in `bd ready` output alongside actual work items.

## Problem
`bd ready` is meant to show work available for polecats to claim. MRs are internal workflow items processed by the Refinery, not polecat work.

## Example
```
| P1 | merge-request | bd-3zzh | Merge: bd-tvu3 |
| P1 | merge-request | bd-fcl1 | Merge: bd-au0.5 |
```

## Possible Fixes
1. **Filter by type** - `bd ready` excludes type=merge-request, type=gate, type=molecule
2. **Set assignee on submit** - MR assigned to refinery/ when created, so not 'unassigned'
3. **Set status=in_progress** - MR starts in_progress since it's queued for processing

## Related
- gate beads also showing (bd-hyp6, bd-wu62) - same issue","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-23T14:38:29.507419-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T14:38:29.507419-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-82y","title":"Design: Swarm shutdown and worker cleanup","description":"Design for graceful swarm shutdown, worker cleanup, and session cycling.

## Key Decisions

1. Pre-kill verification uses model intelligence (not framework rules)
2. Witness can request restart when context filling (mail self, exit)
3. Mayor NOT involved in per-worker cleanup (Witness responsibility)
4. Clear responsibility boundaries between Mayor/Witness/Polecat

## Subtasks (implementation)

- gt-sd6: Polecat decommission checklist prompting
- gt-f8v: Witness pre-kill verification protocol
- gt-eu9: Witness session cycling and handoff
- gt-gl2: Mayor vs Witness cleanup responsibilities

**Design complete.** Each subtask has full specification in its description.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:47:44.936374-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T20:49:22.849598-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-15T20:12:05.441911-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-83k0","title":"mol-witness-patrol molecule definition","description":"Create mol-witness-patrol in builtin_molecules.go.

## Steps (10 total)
1. inbox-check - Process witness mail (lifecycle, help requests)
2. load-state - Read handoff bead, get nudge counts
3. survey-workers - gt polecat list, categorize by status
4. inspect-workers - tmux capture-pane for each 'working' polecat
5. decide-actions - Apply nudge matrix, queue actions
6. execute-actions - Nudge, kill, or escalate as decided
7. save-state - Update handoff bead with new states
8. generate-summary - Summarize cycle for digest
9. context-check - Check own context usage
10. burn-or-loop - Squash wisp, then loop or cycle session

## Key Behaviors
- Uses wisp storage (.beads-wisp/)
- Reads/writes witness handoff bead for state persistence
- Progressive nudging (3 levels before escalate)
- Pre-kill verification before killing polecats

## Reference
- See prompts/roles/witness.md for protocol details
- See mol-refinery-patrol for similar structure
- Parent epic: gt-aqd8","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/furiosa","created_at":"2025-12-22T16:42:43.697249-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T23:34:29.174842-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T23:34:29.174842-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented mol-witness-patrol with 10 steps in builtin_molecules.go"}

When a crew worker's context fills up (>80%), they should:
1. Write handoff mail to themselves
2. Signal session end
3. System auto-spawns successor session
4. Successor reads handoff, finds attached work, auto-continues

This completes the autonomous overnight work pattern from gt-9g82.

- Session spawning mechanism (tmux/daemon)

 {"id":"gt-ov2","title":"Refinery agent: merge queue processing loop","description":"The Refinery agent processes the merge queue, merging polecat work to main.

## Interface

```go
type Refinery struct {
    rig    *Rig
    queue  *MergeQueue
    git    *Git
    mail   *Mailbox
    config RefineryConfig
}

type RefineryConfig struct {
    AutoMerge     bool          // Auto-merge passing MRs
    RunTests      bool          // Run tests before merge
    TestCommand   string        // Command to run tests
    RequireReview bool          // Require review before merge (future)
}

func NewRefinery(rig *Rig, ...) *Refinery

// Lifecycle
func (r *Refinery) Start() error
func (r *Refinery) Stop() error
func (r *Refinery) IsRunning() bool

// Processing
func (r *Refinery) ProcessQueue() error
func (r *Refinery) ProcessMR(mr *MergeRequest) error
```

## Processing Loop

1. Check queue for pending MRs
2. For each pending MR:
   a. Fetch polecat branch
   b. Attempt merge to refinery/rig (local main)
   c. Run tests if configured
   d. If pass: push to origin, mark merged
   e. If fail: mark rejected, notify polecat
3. Sleep, repeat

## Merge Strategy

- Fast-forward when possible
- Merge commit when not
- On conflict: reject MR, polecat must rebase

## Test Integration

If tests configured:
```bash
cd refinery/rig
git merge polecat/branch
<test_command>  # e.g., go test ./...
```
Result determines merge/reject.

## Notifications

On merge success:
- Mail to polecat: "Your work merged"
- Update bead if issue tracked

On merge failure:
- Mail to polecat: "Merge failed: <reason>"
- Include conflict details if applicable

## Dependencies

Needs: Rig management, Git wrapper, Mail system, Merge queue","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:08.498771-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T14:20:35.032221-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T14:20:35.032221-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-ov2","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:21.801826-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-ov2","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:21.89716-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"gt-ox67","title":"Maintenance: Regular cleanup of closed MR/gate beads","description":"Closed merge-request and gate beads accumulate over time. Need regular cleanup.

## Cleanup Candidates
- type=merge-request with status=closed
- type=gate with status=closed
- type=molecule with status=closed (completed workflows)

## Options
1. **Deacon patrol step** - squash closed MRs periodically
2. **Refinery post-merge** - close and tombstone MR after successful merge
3. **bd doctor check** - warn when stale MR count exceeds threshold

## Consideration
Keep some audit trail? Or just tombstone after N days?","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T14:38:31.532094-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T14:38:31.532094-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-p9zh","title":"gt doctor: detect orphaned code on beads-sync branch","description":"After merging beads-sync to main, some code changes can be lost if the merge conflict resolution drops files.

Add a doctor check that runs:
  git diff main..beads-sync -- '*.go' '*.md'

If there are differences in code files (not just .beads/), warn about potentially orphaned work.

Today's incident: Merge 96c773f lost mailbox.go and router.go changes from 5791752, requiring re-implementation.

Acceptance:
- gt doctor warns if beads-sync has unmerged code changes
- Excludes .beads/ directory (expected to differ)
- Shows file list of orphaned changes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T22:01:56.794648-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T22:19:59.777921-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T22:19:59.777921-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented both checks: persistent-role-branches and beads-sync-orphans. +{"id":"gt-tnss","title":"Analysis: Beads database size and hygiene strategy","description":"Audit beads database size and plan medium-term hygiene.

## Questions
1. How many issues total (including tombstones)?
2. Size of issues.jsonl?
3. Growth rate per day/week?
4. What percentage are tombstones?

## Hygiene Topics
- Compaction: squash tombstones periodically
- Archival: move old closed issues to archive file
- Pruning: delete truly stale items (test beads, old MRs)
- Sync efficiency: large JSONL = slow sync

## Action Items
- [ ] Count total issues by status
- [ ] Count tombstones
- [ ] Measure file sizes
- [ ] Propose hygiene schedule","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T14:38:32.92481-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T14:38:32.92481-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-tnw","title":"MCP beads tools fail from polecat directories","description":"The beads MCP plugin (plugin:beads:beads) fails to find issues when invoked from polecat directories.

- Polecat in /gt/gastown/polecats/dementus
- MCP tool: plugin:beads:beads - show (issue_id: 'gt-th7')
- Error: 'bd command failed: Error: no issue found matching gt-th7'

## Expected
MCP tools should work the same as running bd directly.

## Note
This may be related to gt-tmm (beads sync issue) rather than an MCP-specific bug.  {"id":"gt-tr3d","title":"Zombie session detection for refinery/witness","description":"A Claude session can crash or hang while still appearing as 'running' in tmux. The current IsRunning checks only verify tmux session exists, not that Claude is actually processing.

## Problem
- Refinery session might be hung (Claude crashed, infinite loop, etc.)
- tmux HasSession returns true, so auto-start doesn't trigger
- MRs pile up with no processing

## Solution Options
1. **Activity-based detection**: Check last output timestamp in tmux
2. **Heartbeat file**: Agents write timestamp to file, check staleness
3. **Prompt detection**: Look for Claude '>' prompt indicating idle/stuck

## Related
- gt-bjft (spawn auto-start) assumes IsRunning means healthy
- Witness patrol assumes running refinery is processing","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T01:02:51.089112-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T14:27:08.051538-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-tr3d","depends_on_id":"gt-bjft","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T01:03:12.10595-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
